vulnerabilities.description: AUTHENTICATION BYPASS: isAllowedOrigin() returns true for missing origin headers. Requests without Origin header bypass CORS checks entirely, allowing unauthorized access from any source.

ENVIRONMENT VARIABLE LEAKAGE: ENV_PASSTHROUGH_PREFIXES allows all vars starting with 'CLAUDE_', 'NODE_', 'NPM_' etc. to be passed to child processes. This could leak API keys, tokens, or other secrets if they use these prefixes (e.g., CLAUDE_API_KEY).
PATH TRAVERSAL: Directory traversal check via startsWith() can be bypassed using symlinks pointing outside workspace. An attacker could create symlinks to access files outside intended directory.
MISSING CSRF PROTECTION: State-changing endpoints (POST, PUT, DELETE) accept requests without CSRF tokens. Combined with CORS bypass, this allows cross-site attacks.
INSUFFICIENT INPUT VALIDATION: Model name validation allows forward slashes (regex: /^[a-zA-Z0-9._:/-]+$/). This could enable path traversal or command injection in model selection logic.
WEAK ARTIFACT ID SANITIZATION: Simple regex replacement (replace /[^a-zA-Z0-9\-_]/g) may not prevent all directory traversal vectors when combined with other path operations.
WEAK AUTH TOKEN STORAGE: Token stored in plaintext JSON at ~/.zana/auth.json with mode 0o600. While file permissions are restrictive, the token itself is not encrypted or hashed.

INSUFFICIENT ARGUMENT SANITIZATION: sanitizeArg() only removes control characters (\x00-\x1f, \x7f). Doesn't prevent other injection vectors like quotes, backticks, or semicolons in processed contexts.
RACE CONDITION: Multiple concurrent processes can read/write checkpoint files without locking. Could cause data corruption or state inconsistency.
WEAK FILE PERMISSIONS: Memory file written without explicit mode, relying on default umask. Could be world-readable depending on system configuration.
WEAK FILE PERMISSIONS: Session files written without explicit mode. If umask allows, session data could be readable by other users.

recommendations: FIX HIGH: Reject requests with missing Origin header — change isAllowedOrigin() to return false when origin is null/empty
FIX HIGH: Use environment variable allowlist instead of prefix-based passthrough. Only pass explicitly required vars to child processes
FIX HIGH: Replace symlink-vulnerable path check with fs.realpath() to resolve symlinks before validation
ADD: Implement CSRF tokens for all state-changing API endpoints (POST/PUT/DELETE). Use double-submit-cookie or synchronizer-token pattern
FIX: Validate model names against a whitelist instead of regex. Remove forward slash from allowed characters
FIX: Use crypto.randomBytes(16) for artifact IDs instead of user-provided IDs; sanitize file paths with path.basename()
ADD: Encrypt auth tokens at rest (use libsodium/TweetNaCl). Store hash of token, not plaintext
ADD: Implement file-based locking (flock/fcntl) for checkpoint read/write operations to prevent race conditions
FIX: Write memory and session files with explicit 0o600 mode: fs.writeFileSync(path, data, {mode: 0o600})
ADD: Implement input validation framework at API boundary. Use a schema validator (e.g., zod/joi) for all request bodies
AUDIT: Review all exec/spawn calls — the github-safe.js correctly uses execFileSync, but verify all child process calls do the same
ADD: Implement rate limiting on /agents POST endpoint to prevent denial-of-service via rapid agent spawning
